scenario: Sable Retailer runs a Camunda 8.8 batch refund process where a multi-instance activity creates one refund per item in an input collection, producing 1200 invoices per day across 6 merchants. Every line item must be individually reverted when a compensation throw event fires in this scope.
question: How is compensation invoked for a multi-instance activity by default, and how can per-item reversal be achieved?

options.text: By default the compensation handler of a multi-instance activity is invoked only once after all instances complete, and per-item reversal is achieved by also marking the compensation handler as multi-instance

By default the compensation handler of a multi-instance activity is invoked once per item in the input collection, and per-item reversal is the default behaviour for any compensation handler in this scope
By default the compensation handler of a multi-instance activity is invoked once per item that completed successfully, and per-item reversal of failed items needs an extra retry policy configured
By default the compensation handler of a multi-instance activity is invoked once per active instance, and per-item reversal is achieved by adding a parallel gateway in front of the handler activity

a.text: Correct. The Compensating multi-instance activities section states the handler is invoked only once for the multi-instance activity, and to revert each item separately the compensation handler itself should be marked as multi-instance as well.

b.text: Incorrect. The default is a single invocation that reverts all instances together; per-item invocation requires marking the handler itself as multi-instance, not the original activity alone.

c.text: Incorrect. The number of invocations does not depend on item success or failure; by default the handler is invoked exactly once after all instances of the multi-instance activity have completed.

d.text: Incorrect. Per-item reversal is configured by marking the compensation handler as multi-instance, not by adding a parallel gateway in front of the handler activity in the scope.

explanation: A multi-instance activity invokes its compensation handler only once, after all its instances complete. To revert each item individually, mark the compensation handler itself as multi-instance, typically over the same input collection as the original activity.
